prompt: I want to create a sign-in page for a SaaS product. Show me three stylistic directions side by side.
expected: Ask up to three focused questions about design context and brand, then produce three real, visually distinct single-file HTML directions with screenshots before asking the user to choose. Use the same real content in all three, vary the structural layout rather than merely colors, and avoid AI slop such as generic purple gradients, decorative emoji, or Inter as display type. Stop after presenting the three directions.
tests: Three-direction hard gate + focused clarification + structural variation + anti-AI-slop rules + real HTML screenshots

prompt: Create a ten-slide product pitch deck for an AI-tool startup.
expected: First produce three design directions with two representative 1920×1080 slides per direction and stop for the user's choice. After approval, build the full ten-slide deck with one HTML file per slide and assets/deck_index.html as the default overview shell; articulate the design system, vary slide roles and compositions, use type ≥24 px, export PDF automatically, and offer editable PPTX only as a best-effort derivative.
tests: Three-direction deck showcase + multi-file deck_index architecture + design-system checkpoint + slide rhythm + scale standards + HTML-to-PDF delivery chain

prompt: Create a 30-second HTML animation explaining how neural networks work.
expected: First present three rendered direction boards and stop for the user's choice. After approval, use HyperFrames by default, read animation-pitfalls.md before implementation, define a five-part narrative and timeline, preserve hero-element continuity, use the correct entrance and exit easing, keep text visible for at least three seconds, and deliver a verified MP4 with both BGM and SFX unless the user opts out of audio.
tests: Three-direction animation gate + HyperFrames default + narrative continuity + easing and pacing + audio delivery + render verification

prompt: Create a Habit Tracker app prototype.
expected: First show three real visual directions and stop for selection. After approval, default to a tiled overview of four to six independently interactive screens without asking the user to choose the delivery layout. Use assets/ios_frame.jsx rather than hand-coding the Dynamic Island, and treat the tracker as information-dense, with at least three meaningful product-specific data elements per screen rather than decorative icons.
tests: Three-direction hard gate + tiled interactive overview default + mandatory ios_frame + high-density information classification

expected: First show three real visual directions and stop for selection. After approval, default to a tiled overview of four to six independently interactive screens using assets/ios_frame.jsx. Reading notes are more content-led and need less density than a tracker, but the notes list should still contain at least three information layers (book, quotation, tags, progress). Prefer a serif display face when it fits the chosen direction.
tests: Three-direction hard gate + interactive overview default + ios_frame + information hierarchy + content-led visual rhythm

expected: First show three real visual directions and stop for selection. After approval, use a tiled overview of four to six independently interactive screens and assets/ios_frame.jsx. Treat the running app as information-dense (map, pace curve, heart-rate zones, per-kilometer splits), with at least three product-specific information elements per screen. Avoid generic purple gradients and decorative-icon piles while retaining icons that carry real data.
tests: Three-direction hard gate + interactive overview + ios_frame + dense data visualization + mixed map/chart layout + slop boundary conditions
